## What has been established

The result is a specific bridge, not a completed quantum ontology.

The bridge begins with ordinary quantum mechanics. The state $\Psi$ remains the phase-bearing amplitude object. The operator $\hat{\mathbf p}=-i\hbar\nabla$ remains the generator of spatial translations and the quantum representation of directed momentum. The Schrödinger form remains the phase-evolution law: $i\hbar\partial_t$ reads the energy-unit generator of the state's phase change.

The M1 shift is the interpretation of the generator beneath that familiar form. The primary object is not energy as a primitive scalar. It is core momentum. The flat first-order operator

$$
\hat M=\beta p_f+\boldsymbol\alpha\cdot\hat{\mathbf p}
$$

is the linear quantum package of the inertial relation

$$
M^2=p_f^2+p^2.
$$

This explains why the Dirac form belongs naturally in M1. It is the first-order operator form that keeps fermic scale and directed bosic momentum visible while preserving the scalar magnitude relation. The ordinary nonrelativistic Schrödinger equation then appears as the low-speed envelope equation after the rest-scale phase has been factored out.

The gravity extension is equally important. The stationary operator

$$
\hat M_g=\beta p_{f,c}+\boldsymbol\alpha\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\Pi}
$$

with energy-unit companion $\hat H_g=c\hat M_g$ is compatible with the gravity-chapter bookkeeping because its local principal scalar reading is

$$
M_{g,loc}=\sqrt{p_{f,c}^2+\gamma^{ij}\Pi_i\Pi_j}.
$$

That is the same local magnitude used in stationary observer bookkeeping. The operator does not bypass gravity; it lands on the gravity-side local readout in the classical limit and reduces to the flat operator when the gravitational fields vanish.

The controlled difference is equally clear. M1 uses core momentum as the primary generator, lets stationary scalar gravity dress the internal scale $p_f\to p_{f,c}$, and therefore gives strong scalar wells a direct channel into phase evolution, local envelope dynamics, and tunneling exponents. This does not discard classical free-fall correspondence. It narrows the assumption that gravity can always be removed from local quantum internal-scale physics by choosing a freely falling frame.

Several boundaries remain. The bridge above is not yet a full quantum field theory. It is not a complete measurement theory. It does not require torus geometry, electromagnetic projectors, or source-specific phenomenology as part of the mainline argument.

What has been earned is narrower and cleaner: M1 can speak ordinary quantum mechanics in momentum-first language; it can identify $\hat M$ as the core momentum operator; it can explain the linear and Schrödinger forms as phase-generator structure; it can deform the same operator in a way that is compatible with the stationary gravity machinery already developed in the gravity chapter; and it can state why strong gravity may enhance quantum tunneling through the contextual fermic scale.
